content.full_text:
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
Technical Assistcnce 
(916) 322·5662 
Rosie Vandergriff 
Administration 
322·5660 
Accounting Department 
Murdoch, Mockler & Associates 
1130 K Street, Suite 210 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Rosie: 
Executive/legal 
322·5901 
Enforcement 
322·6441 
July 8, 1983 
Statements cf Economic Interest 
322-6444 
Re: 
Advice No. A-83-170 
This is in reply to your letter dated June 28, 1983, and to 
confirm our previous conversation regarding the proper method of 
reporting payments received for lobbying services by the firm of 
Murdoch, Mockler & Associates from the Los Angeles Unified School 
District (LAUSD). 
You indicated that the LAUSD cannot contract with a firm but 
can only contract with individuals and that, therefore, the 
payments to Murdoch, Mockler & Associates are made to the firm's 
lObbyist. 
Previously, the payments were made to John Mockler; 
currently they are being made to James Murdoch. 
Mr. Murdoch is 
then remitting to Murdoch, Mockler the payment received from LAUSD. 
Because Mr. Murdoch is receiving direct payments from LAUSD, 
he must report receipt of the payments on his Lobbyist Report (Form 
620) in Part II, Column A -- "Payments Received in Connection with 
Lobbying Activity/Salaries, Fees and Retainers," and Mr. Murdoch 
must list LAUSD as the employer. 
In order to clarify that he is 
not personally keeping the payments received from LAUSD, he may 
enter a notation explaining that he is receiving the payments 
directly from LAUSD and is then turning the payments over to the 
firm. 
The firm must report receipt of the payments from Mr. 
Murdoch on its Form 680 -
"Payments Received by Business Entities 
Retained to Lobby," showing Mr. Murdoch as the person making the 
payment. 
On the firm's Form 680, you may include an explanation 
that the payment received from Mr. Murdoch is actually a payment to 
the lobbyist from LAUSD that is being given to the firm. 
Regarding the letter from the Secretary of State's office, you and 
I previously discussed the proper method of correcting the problem 
which exists because of Mr. Mockler's receipt of the payments from 
LAUSD. 
You indicated that Mr. Mockler would amend his Registration 


Rosie Vandergriff 
July 8, 1983 
Page 2 
Statement to show LAUSD as a direct employer, and would amend his 
Lobbyist Report (Form 620) to show receipt of the payments directly 
from LAUSD. 
In addition, Murdoch, Mockler & Associates' Form 680 
will be amended to snow receipt of the payment in question from Mr. 
Mockler, rather than from LAUSD. 
Again, all of these entires on 
the LObbyist and Lobbyist Employer Reports may be fully explained 
in footnotes. 
After discussing this unusual situation with other staff, we 
have determined that this method of disclosure provides the most 
accurate and clear information about the transactions. 
I realize 
that this method varies somewhat from that which we previously 
discussed, so please give me a call if it is not clear, or if you 
have any additional questions. 
cc: Political Reform Division 
Secretary of State's Office 
JP/bb 
Sincerely, 
Jeanne Pritchard 
Political Reform Consultant 


MURDOCH, MOCKLER & ASSOCIATES 
JAMES L.... MURDOCt-i 
DAV!D L. WAL~ATH 
June 28, 1983 
Jean PrItchard 
FaIr Pol itical PractIces Commission

P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 95804 
Dear Jean: 
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 
Pursuant to our phone conversation last this week, this letter wil I hopefully 
clear up the confusIon created by the reportIng of monies received by Murdoch, 
Mockler & Associates from the Los Angeles Unified School District. 
Los Angeles Unified School DTstrlct (LAUSD) has a contract with James L. 
Murdoch (unti I January 31, 1983, that contract was with John Mockler, who is no 
longer with Murdoch, Mockler & Associates). 
My understanding is that LAUSD 
cannot draw up a contract with a firm; all their contracts have to be with 
individuals. 
Therefore, even though LAUSD makes out its checks to the i nd i v i aua I (James L. 
Murdoch), al I monIes received are deposited Into the Murdoch, Mockler & 
Associates account. The lobbyist James L. Murdoch is merely acting as an 
intermediary for LAUSD in transferring the payment from LAUSD to Murdoch, 
Mockl er & Associ ates. 
If you need any further information, please feel free to cal f -- It does get 
quIte confusIng! 
Sincerely,

IVISION

April 26, 1983 
Mr. David W. Koch, Deputy Controller 
Los Angeles Unified Scheel District 
l;.50 Nett;} Grand sA. venue 
··Los Angeles, CA 90012 
Dear Mr. Koch: 
lll"y 
m.~ 
Thank you for your Form 660L, executed on October 19, 1982, for the reporting 
period .July 1, 1982 through September 30, 1982. As required by the PoE tical 
Reform Act of 1974, we have reviewed your report to determine whether it 
conforms on its face with the disclosure requirements. 
Part II-Individual Lobbvist Employed 
On Part II, the name of John Mockler is listed. Our understanding is that 
you retain the firm of Murdock, Mockler &: Associates, Inc. Therefore, 
in the future, please list the firm together with any attributable payments 
on Part III-Payments, Section B: Other Payments to Influence LegislativE:: 
or Administrative Action. 
If you have any questions, please call Debra McCastle at (916) 322-0392. 
Sincerely, 
~~AJ.' 
MARIAN ASH, Assistant Chief 
Political Reform Division

sections.conclusion: Because Mr. Murdoch is receiving direct payments from LAUSD, he must report receipt of the payments on his Lobbyist Report (Form 620) in Part II, Column A -- "Payments Received in Connection with Lobbying Activity/Salaries, Fees and Retainers," and Mr. Murdoch must list LAUSD as the employer. He may enter a notation explaining that he is receiving the payments directly from LAUSD and is then turning the payments over to the firm. The firm must report receipt of the payments from Mr. Murdoch on its Form 680 - "Payments Received by Business Entities Retained to Lobby," showing Mr. Murdoch as the person making the payment. The firm's Form 680 may include an explanation that the payment received from Mr. Murdoch is actually a payment to the lobbyist from LAUSD that is being given to the firm.
sections.question_synthetic: How should Murdoch, Mockler & Associates report lobbying payments received from the Los Angeles Unified School District (LAUSD) when payments are made directly to individual lobbyists (John Mockler and James Murdoch) but then remitted to the firm?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that the individual lobbyist receiving direct payments from LAUSD must report them on Form 620 with LAUSD as employer, and the firm must report receiving the funds from the individual lobbyist on Form 680, with explanatory notations permitted to clarify the payment flow.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes incoming request letter dated June 28, 1983 from Rosie Vandergriff, the FPPC response letter dated July 8, 1983 from Jeanne Pritchard, and a related Secretary of State letter dated April 26, 1983. Parsed as advice letter with specific Q&C content derived from the FPPC response.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: How should Murdoch, Mockler & Associates report lobbying payments received from the Los Angeles Unified School District (LAUSD) when payments are made directly to individual lobbyists (John Mockler and James Murdoch) but then remitted to the firm?

CONCLUSION: Because Mr. Murdoch is receiving direct payments from LAUSD, he must report receipt of the payments on his Lobbyist Report (Form 620) in Part II, Column A -- "Payments Received in Connection with Lobbying Activity/Salaries, Fees and Retainers," and Mr. Murdoch must list LAUSD as the employer. He may enter a notation explaining that he is receiving the payments directly from LAUSD and is then turning the payments over to the firm. The firm must report receipt of the payments from Mr. Murdoch on its Form 680 - "Payments Received by Business Entities Retained to Lobby," showing Mr. Murdoch as the person making the payment. The firm's Form 680 may include an explanation that the payment received from Mr. Murdoch is actually a payment to the lobbyist from LAUSD that is being given to the firm.

You indicated that the LAUSD cannot contract with a firm but can only contract with individuals and that, therefore, the payments to Murdoch, Mockler & Associates are made to the firm's lObbyist. Previously, the payments were made to John Mockler; currently they are being made to James Murdoch. Mr. Murdoch is then remitting to Murdoch, Mockler the payment received from LAUSD. Because Mr. Murdoch is receiving direct payments from LAUSD, he must report receipt of the payments on his Lobbyist Report (Form 620) in Part II, Column A -- "Payments Received in Connection with Lobbying Activity/Salaries, Fees and Retainers," and Mr. Murdoch must list LAUSD as the employer. In order to clarify that he is not personally keeping the payments received from LAUSD, he may enter a notation explaining that he is receiving the payments directly from LAUSD and is then turning the payments over to the firm. The firm must report receipt of the payments from Mr. Murdoch on its Form 680 - "Payments Received by Business Entities Retained to Lobby," showing Mr. Murdoch as the person making the payment. On the firm's Form 680, you may include an explanation that the payment received from Mr. Murdoch is actually a payment to the lobbyist from LAUSD that is being given to the firm. Regarding the letter from the Secretary of State's office, you and I previously discussed the proper method of correcting the problem which exists because of Mr. Mockler's receipt of the payments from LAUSD. You indicated that Mr. Mockler would amend his Registration Rosie Vandergriff July 8, 1983 Page 2 Statement to show LAUSD as a direct employer, and would amend his Lobbyist Report (Form 620) to show receipt of the payments directly from LAUSD. Again, all of these entires on the LObbyist and Lobbyist Employer Reports may be fully explained in footnotes. After discussing this unusual situation with other staff, we have determined that this method of disclosure provides the most accurate and clear information about the transactions. I realize that this method varies somewhat from that which we previously discussed, so please give me a call if it is not clear, or if you have any additional questions. cc: Political Reform Division Secretary of State's Office JP/bb Sincerely, Jeanne Pritchard Political Reform
embedding.summary: This FPPC advice letter addresses the proper reporting method for lobbying payments from LAUSD to Murdoch, Mockler & Associates, which flow through individual lobbyists. The FPPC provides specific guidance on how payments should be reported on Forms 620 and 680 with appropriate notations to clarify the transaction structure.
